jokes.text: Armadale, for shizzle.
jokes.setup: Dave asking about Jay-Z's vodka brand name
jokes.punchline: Adding 'for shizzle' to the sophisticated vodka name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective juxtaposition of high-end product with street slang

jokes.text: Girl, I got something that'll keep your flow motherfucking tiz-ight!
jokes.punchline: Applying hip-hop slang to menstrual products
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - applying rap mogul marketing to feminine hygiene

jokes.text: Roc-A-Fella's about to get all up in that ass... with Roca-pads.
jokes.punchline: Applied to feminine hygiene products
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of rap marketing aggression applied to absurd context - expertly structured

jokes.text: So if your period is droppin', then, we catch it
jokes.punchline: Applied to menstrual flow
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd concept with perfect rap vernacular adaptation

jokes.text: Ooh, for shizzle, my bizzle.
jokes.punchline: Adopts the rap slang after using rap-branded pads
jokes.explanation: Logical extension of the sketch premise but not as surprising as the setup

jokes.text: So, if your bum is leakin', you need to be seekin'... Roca-pads.
jokes.punchline: About feminine hygiene issues
jokes.explanation: Good continuation with rhyming structure typical of rap marketing

jokes.text: It's real shitty down here. Oh, my... it's Redman.
jokes.punchline: Redman appearing in toilet bowl
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd reveal - Redman as toilet bowl spokesperson is perfect character integration

jokes.text: My shit is da bomb, bitch.
jokes.punchline: Literal 'shit' product being 'da bomb'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - double meaning of 'shit' as both product and slang for 'stuff'

jokes.text: the enzymes and shit collaborate together, and make some kind of mixture
jokes.setup: Trying to explain toilet cleaner scientifically
jokes.punchline: Using street slang and vague terminology for chemistry
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Redman's street persona trying to sound scientific

jokes.text: you got celery and lima beans and corn and chicken bones and shit in here. It was yummy going down.
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic 'it was yummy going down' about waste
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but cleverly structured - the nostalgic callback to eating makes it funnier

jokes.text: Flush the toilet with your pancake ass, bitch!
jokes.punchline: Breaking character with random insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but 'pancake ass' feels somewhat random

jokes.text: Hey, Redman Toilet Freshener, I'll get the brown out.
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - stains and racial undertone
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with multiple meanings, good button for the commercial

jokes.text: I'd been on an eight-week losing streak, man. I needed to pull out a win and I needed it then.
jokes.punchline: Applied to picking up women
jokes.explanation: Solid premise setup - treating hookups like sports statistics

jokes.text: Ashley Evans, affectionately known throughout the league as 'Smashly' Evans
jokes.punchline: Nickname suggesting promiscuity
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect sports documentary style delivery

jokes.text: And I would like a man.
jokes.punchline: Ordering a man like it's on the menu
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - perfect economy with clear setup/punchline

jokes.text: Can anybody take this drunk, horny, crazy woman home?
jokes.setup: Bartender taking away keys for safety
jokes.punchline: Basically auctioning off drunk woman
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but relies somewhat on problematic premise

jokes.text: It was Jimmy Mackey's 9th interception of the year, living up to his reputation as one of the bad boys of the league.
jokes.punchline: Applied to guy stealing Johnson's pickup target
jokes.explanation: Excellent sports metaphor execution - 'interception' works perfectly for stealing a pickup

jokes.text: Hey, hey, you forgot your scarf. But it wasn't her scarf. Young Ashley didn't even own a scarf, but was too drunk to realize it.
jokes.setup: Johnson using pickup line about forgotten scarf
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's not her scarf and she's too drunk to know
jokes.explanation: Great structure - setup, reveal, and additional commentary that makes it funnier

jokes.text: Man, of all the flights to be on, I gotta ride with them terrorist sons of bitches. I got my eye on you, Al Qaeda.
jokes.punchline: Black passenger racially profiling Middle Eastern passengers
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary - turning racial profiling on its head by having the usually profiled do the profiling

jokes.text: What are those negroes doing in first class? Must be rappers. I'd better keep an eye on Sara.
jokes.punchline: Assuming black people in first class are rappers and protecting his wife
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of racial profiling theme with specific stereotypes

jokes.text: Me no trust-em white man. Me better not go to bathroom. White man will steal my seat and call it 'Manifest Destiny'.
jokes.punchline: Connecting airplane seat theft to historical land theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical callback - connecting mundane airplane anxiety to genocide with perfect economy

jokes.text: You don't want to alienate yourself or, maybe, get beat the fuck up.
jokes.setup: White people having questions for Black friends
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violence as consequence
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the segment with casual escalation to violence

jokes.text: Yeah, black people can jump high. You gotta jump, gotta do something when you're running from the police.
jokes.setup: Stereotypical question about black athletic ability
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to police brutality
jokes.explanation: Classic Mooney - takes innocent stereotype and gives dark social commentary twist

jokes.text: Everybody wanna be a nigger, but nobody wanna be a nigger, how about that question?
jokes.punchline: Paradox of wanting black culture without black experience
jokes.explanation: Legendary line - perfectly captures cultural appropriation paradox with devastating economy and insight

jokes.text: Carol Channing just admitted she was a nigger, the rest of 'em need to break down and admit it too.
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Carol Channing's recent racial revelation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with topical reference that extends the previous brilliant observation

jokes.text: Well, I have a question for that nigga. Well, where are your teeth, nigga?
jokes.setup: Person asking about black drug use
jokes.punchline: Turning it around to question asker's missing teeth
jokes.explanation: Good reversal technique - answering question with more pointed question about obvious visual

jokes.text: And don't ask me about drugs, ask Whitney and Bobby, don't ask me that question.
jokes.setup: Deflecting question about black drug use
jokes.punchline: Pointing to specific celebrities known for drug issues
jokes.explanation: Solid deflection with topical reference, though somewhat predictable target

jokes.text: Nigger, I am gonna get cancelled for sure.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about show's controversial content
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - acknowledging the show's boundary-pushing content

jokes.text: Tell 'em I got shot by some Chinese gangsters. I can describe 'em.
jokes.punchline: Casual racism and offer to describe attackers
jokes.explanation: Decent character comedy but relies somewhat on racial stereotype for humor

jokes.text: You might as well just shoot yourself in the stomach and come lay down with me. They're gonna see you.
jokes.setup: Dramatic war scene with sneaking soldier
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion for fellow soldier to shoot himself
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic delivered casually in dramatic moment

jokes.text: Permission to die, Sarge. Permission granted. You report to heaven at 0-800 hours, and that's an order, soldier.
jokes.punchline: Treating death as military assignment
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist premise treating death as military duty with specific time

jokes.text: Don't ask God... ask Nixon. Why, Nixon, why?!
jokes.setup: Classic 'Why, God, why?' dying soldier line
jokes.punchline: Redirecting blame to Nixon instead of God
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of classic war movie trope with political commentary

jokes.text: I'd love some pussy before I go, ahh...
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual request as final words
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that undercuts dramatic moment but feels somewhat cheap

jokes.text: I'd love some pussy before I go, ahh...
jokes.punchline: Sexual desire as last request
jokes.explanation: Crude but honest final wish, somewhat predictable ending

jokes.text: My lady, my lady, don't run from me. My lady... hey!
jokes.punchline: Formal language breaking down to street 'hey!'
jokes.explanation: Good setup of character mixing formal and informal speech

jokes.text: and foundeth her... her birthday present early.
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation that evidence was birthday gift
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but the Shakespeare parody feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: The last maiden that accused me of lying, I stuck my foot betwixt her buttocks so ravagely... That it broketh off and snappeth as the twig doth snap!
jokes.punchline: Absurdly violent and graphic threat in Shakespearean language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd violence delivered in period language

jokes.text: to come and watch me unsheath my sword and swing it and swing it upon thee!
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo with sword metaphor
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo with sword metaphor, not particularly original

jokes.text: and swing it and swing it upon thee!
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo delivered dramatically
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo in period language, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I've soiled my pantaloon. I'm going to get a bowl of cereal.
jokes.punchline: Premature ejaculation and mundane cereal request
jokes.explanation: Excellent anticlimax - perfect deflation from medieval drama to mundane modern need

jokes.text: It seems as though the boudoir, perhaps, won't be necessary. I've soiled my pantaloon.
jokes.punchline: Premature ejaculation confession
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of macho posturing with embarrassing confession

jokes.text: Short foot... Long foot... Little foot... Long foot...
jokes.punchline: Rhythmic description of uneven gait
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that borders on exploitative but has rhythmic appeal

jokes.text: Ahh, get your damned hands off of me! But, sir, the wet floor... Shut up... oh!
jokes.punchline: Immediately slipping after rejecting help
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic structure but somewhat predictable physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Shut your damn mouth, you pothead!
jokes.setup: Person trying to help with skateboard warning
jokes.punchline: Angry handicapped man calling helper a pothead
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor with random insult, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: May I have a double cheeseburger... a large fry... and something cold to drink?
jokes.setup: Character has just been through multiple accidents
jokes.punchline: Calmly ordering food as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan contrast - calmly ordering after chaos shows character resilience

jokes.text: And a cherry pie!
jokes.punchline: Adding dessert with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation that shows character's appetite despite trauma

jokes.text: And a milkshake!
jokes.punchline: Final addition with more enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Continues escalation pattern but becomes somewhat repetitive

jokes.text: If you let one drop hit this motherfuckin' waverunner, I'm a kick your motherfuckin' ass.
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence over water touching a watercraft
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - threatening someone for getting water on a water vehicle

jokes.text: If you let one drop hit this motherfuckin' waverunner, I'm a kick your motherfuckin' ass.
jokes.punchline: Aggressive protection of water vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good Tyrone Biggums character moment with unexpected context

jokes.text: Oh, girl, I should shit on you.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, aggressive threat
jokes.explanation: Typical Tyrone Biggums crude threat, fits character but not exceptional

jokes.text: I should pee on y'all like R. Kelly.
jokes.punchline: R. Kelly character referencing R. Kelly scandal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment - R. Kelly character threatening to act like R. Kelly

jokes.text: I'm rich, biatch!
jokes.punchline: Celebratory catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase with perfect economy, became cultural touchstone
